2152(अ) और दनांक 23.08.2017 के का.आ. 2752(अ) के माdयम से संशोिधत कया गया। MINISTRY OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE (Department of Agriculture, Cooperation and Farmers Welfare) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 6th October, 2017 S.O. 3293 (E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of section 3 of the Destructive Insects and Pests Act, 1914 (2 of 1914), the Central Government hereby makes the following Order further to amend the Plant Quarantine (Regulation of Import into India) Order, 2003, namely:— 1. Short title and Commencement.—(1) This Order may be called the Plant Quarantine (Regulation of Import into India) (Eighth Amendment) Order, 2017. (2) It shall come into force on the date of its publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Amendment of Schedule VI.—In the Schedule VI to the Plant Quarantine (Regulation of Import into India) Order, 2003— (i) Against serial number 382, relating to Nigella sativa seeds (black cumin), the existing entries in column (3) shall be numbered as item (i) ‘Seeds for sowing’, after item (i) as so re-numbered, and the entries relating thereto, in columns (3), (4), (5) and (6) the following item and entries shall respectively be inserted, namely: -- 4 of 6 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(ii)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 5 (3) (4) (5) (6) (ii) Seed for consumption/ Processing Ethiopia Free from: a) Quarantine weed seeds as listed under Schedule VIII of PQ Order, 2003. b) Soil and other plant debris. NIL (ii) Against serial number 775, relating to Chenopodium quinoa (Quinoa) in column (3) against entry ‘Grain/Seeds for consumption/processing’, after item (ii) and the entries relating thereto in columns (4), (5) and (6) the following item and entries relating thereto shall respectively be inserted, namely: (4) (5) (6) (iii) Ecuad or Free from: a) Quarantine weed seeds as listed under Schedule VIII of PQ Order, 2003. b) Soil and other plant debris. NIL (iii) After serial number 779 and the entries relating thereto, the following serial number and entries thereto shall be inserted, namely:- S.No Plant Species Category of plant material Country of origin Additional declarations required to be incorporated into Phytosanitary Certificate Special conditions of import (1) (2) (3) (4) (5) (6) 780 Larix Spp. (Larch) Timber logs with/ without bark for consumption Canada Free from: a) Monochamus scutellatus scutellatus (whites potted sawyer) b) Monochamus scutellatus (whites potted sawyer) c) Otiorhynchus singularis (clay coloured weevil) d) Lachnellula willkommii (European larch canker) e) Dendroctonus simplex (eastern larch beetle) f) Dryocoetes autographus (bark beetle) g) Monochamus scutellatus oregonensis (Oregon fir sawyer) h) Sirex juvencus (steel-blue wood wasp) i) Gnathotirchus sulcatus (western hemlock wood stainer) j) Dendroctonus pseudotsugae (douglas-fir beetle) k) Orgyia leucostigma (white-marked tussock moth) l) Bursaphelenchus xylophilus (pine wilt nematode) m) Orgyia pseudotsugata (douglas-fir tussock moth) n) Trypodendron lineatum (striped ambrosia beetle) o) Ips grandicollis (five-spined bark beetle) Fumigation with methyl bromide at 48 g per cubic metre for 24 hrs at 21oC or above or equivalent thereof; OR Heat Treatment at 560C (core temperature) for 30 minutes. The treatment should be endorsed on the Phytosanitary Certificate issued at the country of origin/re-export. “781 Tectona grandis (Teak) Timber (Sawn or sized wood) Ecuador Free from: a) Coptotermes testaceus (Termite) b) Steirastoma breve (Cocao beetle) The consignment is free from quarantine weed seeds, 1. Export consignment must comply with Systems Approach. 2. Pre-shipment fumigation with phosphine gas @ 3gm per cubic meter (Aluminium phosphide/ Magnesium phosphide) for 7 days. -- 5 of 6 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] 3. Fumigation agency and fumigation operator must be accredited by NPPO India. 3. Amendment of Schedule VII.—In the Schedule VII to the Plant Quarantine (Regulation of Import into India) Order, 2003: After the existing serial numbers 299 and the entries relating thereto, respectively, the following entries shall be inserted namely:— “300. Ceratonia sligua. – Carob dried pods/ seeds for consumption/ medicinal purpose 301 Sambucus niger – Elder berry dried fruits for consumption/ medicinal purpose.” [F.No.8-21/2017-PP.II] ASHWANI KUMAR, Jt.
